title: Dark Mode CSS Fixes for Metric Analyst Page
story: As a data analyst using the Metric Analyst page in dark mode, I want all text, icons, buttons, and gridlines to be properly styled for readability, so that I can work comfortably in my preferred theme.
description:
Multiple CSS tweaks needed on the Metric Analyst page for proper dark mode support.

## Fixes
1. **Value text color**: Change metric values to match the regular body text color
2. **Subtitle headings**: Use regular text color instead of primary color for subtitles (H5 tags globally changed to not use primary color)
3. **Close button**: Fix close button visibility on the merge screen in dark mode
4. **Gridlines**: Apply the same gridline styling as the standard table component
5. **Back button**: Fix back button styling for dark mode
6. **Alerts tab links**: Ensure links are visible in dark mode; timestamps/dates are not styled as clickable
acceptance_criteria: Metric values display in the same text color as body text in dark mode
Subtitle headings use regular text color instead of primary color
Close button on merge screen is visible in dark mode
Gridlines match the standard table component styling
Back button is properly styled in dark mode
Links on the Alerts tab are visible and distinguishable in dark mode
Timestamps and dates on the Alerts tab are not styled as clickable links
ux_changes: Multiple dark mode CSS fixes on the Metric Analyst page: value text colors, subtitle headings changed from primary to regular text color, close button visibility, consistent gridlines, back button styling, and alert tab link visibility.
implementation_notes: H5 tags were globally set to use the primary color, which looked incorrect in dark mode for subtitles. Product owner approved removing primary color from H5 globally. Close button and back button needed explicit dark mode CSS overrides. Alert links visibility may have been a browser cache issue for some items.
comment_summary: Developer addressed most issues. Noted that H5 tags globally used primary color — product owner approved removing it globally. Alert link visibility may have been a cache issue. All fixes deployed to staging with instructions to test in incognito.
